title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Your heart is an extraordinary machine.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: During your lifetime, your heart's muscular walls pump blood into blood vessels, branching

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: throughout your body.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Your heart has four chambers.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Two upper chambers, called the left and right atria, and two lower chambers, called

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: the left and right ventricles, contract in a steady rhythm known as your heart beat.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: During a normal heart beat, blood from your tissues and lungs flows into your atria, then

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: into your ventricles.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Inside your heart, called the inter atrial and interventricular septa, help keep the

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: blood on left and right sides from mixing.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Two valves sit like doors between your atria and ventricles to prevent blood from flowing

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: backward into your atria.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: The tricuspid valve opens into your right ventricle, and the mitral valve opens into your

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: left ventricle.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Strong, thin tissues, called corda tendonae, hold your valves in place during the forceful

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: contractions of your ventricles.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Blood leaving the ventricles passes through another set of valves, the pulmonary valve

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: between your right ventricle and pulmonary trunk, and the aortic valve connecting your

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: left ventricle and aorta.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: In order to pump blood more efficiently, your heart muscle, called myocardium, is arranged

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: in a unique pattern.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Three layers of myocardium wrap around the lower part of your heart.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: They twist and tighten in different directions to push blood through your heart.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: When special cells, called pacemaker cells, generate electrical signals inside your heart.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: The heart muscle cells, called myocytes, contract as a group.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Your heart is divided into left and right halves, which work together like a dual pump.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: On the right side of your heart, deoxygenated blood from your body's tissues flows through

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: large veins, called the superior and inferior vena cava, into your right atrium.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Next, the blood moves into your right ventricle, which contracts and sends blood out of your

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: heart to your lungs to gather oxygen and get rid of carbon dioxide.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: On the left side of your heart, oxygen-rich blood from your lungs flows through your

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: pulmonary veins into your left atrium.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: The blood then moves into your left ventricle, which contracts and sends blood out of your

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: heart through the aorta to feed your cells and tissues.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: The first branches off your aorta are the coronary arteries, which supply your heart muscle

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: with oxygen and nutrients.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: At the top of your aorta, arteries branch off to carry blood to your head and arms.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Arteries branching from the middle and lower parts of your aorta, supply blood to the

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: rest of your body.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Your heart beats an average of 60 to 100 beats per minute.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: In that one minute, your heart pumps about 5 quarts of blood through your arteries,

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: delivering a steady stream of oxygen and nutrients all over your body.
